editableNewDialogue.text: i found two listings: one says “body only, untested,” the other says “tested, includes charger + 1 battery.” is “untested” basically a red flag or can it be fine?

“Untested” is usually a red flag because it often means “I don’t want to say it’s broken.” It *can* be fine, but you should assume higher risk and only consider it if the price is low enough that you’re okay gambling.

If you want to screen it quickly, ask: does it power on, does it take a photo to SD, does the shutter fire consistently, does the sensor look clean (no scratches/oil spots), do dials/buttons respond, and does it charge properly. If they can’t answer basics, treat it as parts/repair.
what’s the quickest way to check a used camera in person so i don’t miss something obvious?

Fast in-person checklist:
1) Power on/off repeatedly (no freezing).
2) Take 5–10 shots at different shutter speeds; review at 100% for weird lines/spots.
3) Point at a plain bright surface (sky/wall) at f/8–f/11 to reveal dust spots.
4) Check buttons, dials, playback, and menu navigation.
5) Test the card slot + USB/HDMI ports if possible.
6) Listen for grinding/rattling; check battery door and mount for looseness.

Unrelated example: some older compact cameras (like early 2010s models) commonly develop sticky zoom barrels—so if anything feels gritty or hesitant, that’s a walk-away sign on many used cams.
if i buy a spare battery, are third-party batteries usually safe or should i only get official?

Third-party batteries can be fine, but quality varies. Safer approach: buy from a reputable brand with protection circuitry and good reviews, and avoid the absolute cheapest no-name packs.

If you’re comparing options, look for: proper capacity ratings (not exaggerated), included decently-built charger, and return policy. As a side note (not about you): some airlines have specific limits on loose lithium batteries in carry-on vs checked baggage, so it’s worth checking rules if anyone travels with spares.
do you think a tripod or a flash gives more “quality improvement” for the money, generally?

Generally: a tripod gives the biggest improvement for sharpness in low light and for consistent framing (night shots, indoor stills, long exposures). A flash gives the biggest improvement for people/photos indoors when light is bad, because it controls the scene’s light rather than just stabilizing.

If the main goal is cleaner, sharper low-light without motion, tripod wins. If the goal is better-looking faces indoors, flash wins. Random example: photojournalists often prefer a small bounce-capable flash over a bigger lens upgrade for event coverage because lighting is the real limiter.

if i end up grabbing the nx1000, im probably gonna shoot mostly street stuff at night so i dont wanna be fighting noise the whole time. also i only really edit on my laptop with lightroom so id need raws that dont fall apart.

Night street is doable on the NX1000, but you’ll want to keep expectations realistic: the APS-C sensor helps versus a phone, yet older sensors can get crunchy once you push shadows hard. If you expose a bit to protect the shadows (without clipping highlights), RAWs from it can still hold up fine in Lightroom—just avoid extreme underexposure. Pairing it with a faster prime (like a 30mm f/2 or 45mm f/1.8 if you find one cheap) matters more than chasing settings; it lets you keep ISO lower and makes the files grade cleaner. If you stick with the kit zoom at night, you’ll feel the limits sooner.
quick correction: i’m not stuck on under 50€ anymore. that was just me hoping for a miracle deal. realistically i can do like 150–200€ if it’s actually worth it.
Got it—so the “under 50€” thing was more of a best-case scenario, not your real budget. If you can stretch to 150–200€, you’ll have way more solid options than the ultra-cheap stuff.
yeah exactly. i’d rather buy one decent thing than chase random 40€ lenses that kinda suck.
That makes sense. With 150–200€ you’re no longer limited to bottom-tier picks, and you’re less likely to waste money on something you’ll replace immediately.
